channel.title: CyberWatch Threat Intelligence
channel.description: Aggregated CVEs, advisories, incidents and IOCs

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: bpfman-0.5.4-13.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-fa34dc95e6)
item.description: Automatic update for bpfman-0.5.4-13.fc45. ##### **Changelog** ``` * Wed Jul 22 2026 Daniel Mellado - 0.5.4-13 - Bump vendored openssl to 0.10.78 / openssl-sys to 0.9.117 for OpenSSL 4.0 support * Wed Jul 15 2026 Fedora Release Engineering - 0.5.4-12 - Rebuilt for https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Fedora_45_Mass_Rebuild * Fri Jun 12 2026 Yaa

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: kernel-7.1.4-104.fc43 (FEDORA-2026-6503a6a639)
item.description: The 7.1.4-104/204 stable kennel updates contain a couple of security fixes for issues with exploits in the wild.

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: kernel-7.1.4-204.fc44 (FEDORA-2026-2b94d8d05c)
item.description: The 7.1.4-104/204 stable kennel updates contain a couple of security fixes for issues with exploits in the wild.

item.description: - Update to latest upstream (153.0) ---- - New upstream release (153.0) ---- - Updated to latest upstream (153.0)

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: libwignernj-0.7.0-6.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-8d270f3d39)
item.description: Automatic update for libwignernj-0.7.0-6.fc45. ##### **Changelog** ``` * Thu Jul 23 2026 Susi Lehtola - 0.7.0-6 - Fix gating on BR: libquadmath-devel. * Thu Jul 23 2026 Susi Lehtola - 0.7.0-5 - Install the Fortran modules into %{_fmoddir} via CMake instead of moving them after the fact, fixing the non-existent include directory

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: python-oracledb-4.0.2-1.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-f75e94cb20)
item.description: Automatic update for python-oracledb-4.0.2-1.fc45. ##### **Changelog** ``` * Wed Jul 22 2026 Packit - 4.0.2-1 - Update to 4.0.2 upstream release - Resolves: rhbz#2500543 * Wed Jul 22 2026 Python Maint - 4.0.1-4 - Rebuilt for Python 3.15.0b4 ABI change ```

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: libgit2_1.9-1.9.6-1.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-7c3fc14617)
item.description: - Update libgit2 to version 1.9.6. - Update the libgit2-sys Rust crate to the corresponding version 0.18.7. This includes fixes for various security issues, including - but not limited to: - [CVE-2026-53583](https://github.com/libgit2/libgit2/security/advisories/GHSA-h7gc-w2gg-p9xp) - [CVE-2026-53584](https://github.com/libgit2/libgit2/security/advisories/GHSA-cw77-j82w-mchm) - [CVE-2026-53585](

item.title: [CRITICAL] Customer CRM Data Accessed in Supply Chain Incident
item.description: LastPass experienced a security incident through Klue, a third-party market intelligence platform integrated with its Salesforce and Gong systems. On June 12, 2026, LastPass was notified that an unauthorized actor exploited stolen OAuth tokens held by Klue to access customer relationship management data within LastPass's Salesforce environment. The exposed information includes customer names, emai

item.title: [MEDIUM] Global Webmail Espionage
item.description: A persistent cyberespionage campaign tracked as CL-STA-1114, overlapping with Russian threat actor activity known as Void Blizzard and LAUNDRY BEAR, has been targeting Zimbra webmail in government, defense, transportation, and financial organizations. The campaign focuses on NATO member states, Ukraine, Commonwealth of Independent States countries, and Africa. Attackers exploit CVE-2025-66376, a v

item.title: [LOW] Large-Scale GitHub Actions Abuse Powers a Distributed cPanel and WHM Exploitation Campaign
item.description: A large-scale campaign exploited GitHub Actions by compromising repositories to launch distributed attacks against cPanel and WHM servers. The operation began with compromised developer accounts, pushing malicious workflow files that executed on GitHub-hosted runners rather than through traditional package installation. These workflows downloaded Linux payloads from command-and-control infrastruct

item.title: [MEDIUM] Zimbra Mailservers Targeted with Half-Click Exploits
item.description: Russian-aligned threat actor TA488, also known as Void Blizzard and Laundry Bear, exploited a previously unknown vulnerability in Zimbra Collaboration Suite mailservers for at least five months during 2025. The vulnerability, later assigned CVE-2025-66376, was leveraged through half-click exploits embedded in HTML emails that executed upon opening without requiring user interaction. Upon successfu

item.title: [CRITICAL] Operation RoundPress Rolls on with More Half-Click Webmail Zero-Days
item.description: TA458, a Russia-aligned espionage group likely linked to GRU, continues exploiting half-click cross-site scripting vulnerabilities in webmail platforms to steal sensitive email data. These exploits require no user interaction beyond opening the malicious email in webmail. The actor exploited multiple zero-days including SOGo (CVE-2026-8496), Zimbra (CVE-2025-27915), and mDaemon (CVE-2025-3929), al

item.title: [MEDIUM] Email threat landscape: Q2 2026 trends and insights
item.description: During Q2 2026, Microsoft detected approximately 7.6 billion email-based phishing threats, with monthly volumes declining from 2.7 billion in April to 2.4 billion in June. The quarter was significantly shaped by the downstream effects of Microsoft's Digital Crimes Unit disruption of the Tycoon2FA phishing-as-a-service platform in March, resulting in a 92% decline in associated phishing volume. QR

item.title: [MEDIUM] Upgrades MaaS Ecosystem with Modular Tools
item.description: Insikt Group identified four new malware families from TAG-195 (Golden Chickens, Venom Spider), a financially motivated malware-as-a-service developer. The families include TinyEgg, a lightweight initial-access backdoor; ChonkyChicken, which expands capabilities with browser credential theft and session automation; a modularized ChonkyChicken variant using controller-and-plugin architecture; and C

item.title: [MEDIUM] Thailand's Ministry of Finance Targeted With Hermes AI Agent Running Unattended, Hades Implant Staged
item.description: Between July 9-13, 2026, three exposed directories on a Hong Kong server revealed an ongoing cyber espionage operation targeting Thailand's Ministry of Finance. The attack leveraged Hermes, an autonomous AI agent operating in unattended YOLO mode, alongside a custom Go-based implant called Hades. Recovered files included exploit code for multiple vulnerabilities, webshells, stolen credentials, and

item.title: [HIGH] Inside FortiBleed: Reverse Engineering the CyberStrike Harvester Behind a Global FortiGate Credential Factory
item.description: FortiBleed is a large-scale credential compromise campaign targeting internet-facing Fortinet FortiGate firewalls and SSL VPN gateways globally. The operation employs a sophisticated credential pipeline utilizing credential stuffing, password spraying, configuration harvesting, offline cracking, and post-authentication capture processing. Reverse engineering of the CyberStrike Harvester v1.5 binar

item.title: [MEDIUM] Skill Marketplace and the Emerging AI Supply Chain Threat
item.description: Between February and May 2026, researchers identified five malicious skills on ClawHub, OpenClaw's AI agent marketplace, that evaded detection by VirusTotal and ClawScan. The threats included two macOS infostealers communicating with command-and-control infrastructure, one skill using file padding to bypass scanner thresholds, and two novel agentic threats exploiting the AI supply chain for financ

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-58057
item.description: Flowise Windows RCE exploit for CVE-2026-58057. Bypasses environment variable validation via case-sensitive flaw. Uses node_options to inject arbitrary code through MCP stdio. Supports reverse shell, persistence, file upload, credential dumping. For authorized security testing only.

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-45132
item.description: GitHub Actions workflow sandbox for CVE-2026-45132 reproduction

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-59880
item.description: finding by nvth

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2011-2523
item.description: Tproot es una máquina de nivel Muy Fácil de DockerLabs centrada en la explotación manual del servicio vsftpd 2.3.4 (CVE-2011-2523).

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2019-5392
item.description: Legacy HPE iMC vuln

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-45585
item.description: YellowKey free tool for the CVE-2026-45585 BitLocker bypass vulnerability on Windows 10/11. Covered on Tom's Hardware: extract recovery keys, apply remediation, test bypass mitigation and manage BitLocker encryption state. Download YellowKey

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2025-9242
item.description: Detection-only asynchronous IPv4 scanner for identifying WatchGuard Fireware version/build profiles associated with CVE-2025-9242 via IKEv2.

item.title: [MEDIUM] PoC released: CVE-2024-6387
item.description: Security analysis and report of CVE-2024-6387 OpenSSH vulnerability, including vulnerability details, CVSS evaluation, and mitigation recommendations.

item.title: [CRITICAL] PoC released: CVE-2026-23744
item.description: Proof-of-concept and offensive security research analyzing CVE-2026-23744 (MCPJam Inspector Unauthenticated RCE, Patched in v1.4.3+).

item.title: [MEDIUM] PoC released: CVE-2026-65971
item.description: Proof-of-concept and technical write-up for CVE-2026-65971 — SQL injection via the sortDirection Livewire property in power-components/livewire-powergrid (< 6.10.4)

item.description: Ransomware group 'qilin' claimed victim: ABM Enviro. Country: CA. Sector: Professional Services.

item.description: Ransomware group 'qilin' claimed victim: Machinerie P&W. Country: CA. Sector: Manufacturing.

item.description: Ransomware group 'killsec' claimed victim: cashcowboy. Country: US. Sector: Financial Services.

item.description: Ransomware group 'cmdorganization' claimed victim: T Simon Jewelers. Country: US. Sector: Retail & E-Commerce.

item.description: Ransomware group 'play' claimed victim: Restaurant Depot. Country: US. Sector: Retail & E-Commerce.

item.description: Ransomware group 'play' claimed victim: Record Go Alquiler. Country: ES. Sector: Hospitality.

item.title: [MEDIUM] Arch Linux: Arch Issue
item.description: Type: | Severity: Unknown | Package:

item.title: [MEDIUM] VMware: VMSA-2026-0004: VMware Cloud Foundation Operations updates address multiple vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-41722, CVE-2026-41723 and CVE-2026-41724)
[MEDIUM] CVE-2026-15669: A vulnerability was found in louisho5 picobot up to 0.2.0. This issue affects th...
item.description: A vulnerability was found in louisho5 picobot up to 0.2.0. This issue affects the function ExecTool.Execute of the file internal/agent/tools/exec.go of the component exec Tool. The manipulation results in os command injection. The attack requires a local approach. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not r

item.title: [LOW] CVE-2026-12988: The WP 2FA WordPress plugin before 3.1.1.2 does not verify that the email addre...
item.description: The WP 2FA WordPress plugin before 3.1.1.2 does not verify that the email address supplied during two-factor authentication setup belongs to the user, allowing an attacker who has obtained a user's credentials to redirect the setup verification code to an attacker-controlled email address and take over the account.

item.title: [CRITICAL] CVE-2026-12583: The Newsletters WordPress plugin before 4.15 does not prevent deserialization of...
item.description: The Newsletters WordPress plugin before 4.15 does not prevent deserialization of untrusted input that is stored through a public form, allowing unauthenticated attackers to inject a PHP object and, via a property-oriented gadget chain bundled with the Newsletters WordPress plugin before 4.15, write arbitrary files and execute code on the server.

item.title: [LOW] CVE-2026-12511: The AI Engine WordPress plugin before 3.5.5 does not sanitize a user-supplied f...
item.description: The AI Engine WordPress plugin before 3.5.5 does not sanitize a user-supplied filename before using it to write a downloaded file, allowing authenticated users with editor-level access to write attacker-controlled bytes to an arbitrary location on the server via path traversal.

item.title: [LOW] CVE-2026-12482: A vulnerability in keras-team/keras version 3.12.0 allows an attacker to craft a...
item.description: A vulnerability in keras-team/keras version 3.12.0 allows an attacker to craft a malicious tar archive that bypasses the `filter_safe_tarinfos` validation in `keras/src/utils/file_utils.py`. Specifically, symlink entries are not subjected to the same `is_path_in_dir` validation as regular file entries, allowing symlinks to be created outside the intended extraction directory. This can lead to syml

item.title: [CRITICAL] CVE-2026-11567: The SureForms WordPress plugin before 2.11.1 does not properly validate the pay...
item.description: The SureForms WordPress plugin before 2.11.1 does not properly validate the payment amount on forms that use a dynamically-sourced (variable/hidden) payment amount, allowing unauthenticated users to underpay for the configured product or subscription. Forms using a fixed configured price are not affected.

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-11563: The Word Count and Social Shares WordPress plugin through 1.0 does not validate ...
item.description: The Word Count and Social Shares WordPress plugin through 1.0 does not validate a user-supplied file path before deletion, nor does it have proper authorization or CSRF checks, allowing any authenticated user, such as a Subscriber, to delete arbitrary files on the server, which can lead to a full site takeover (e.g. by deleting wp-config.php).

item.title: [LOW] CVE-2025-15665: The Ultimate Before After Image Slider & Gallery WordPress plugin before 4.7.1 ...
item.description: The Ultimate Before After Image Slider & Gallery WordPress plugin before 4.7.1 does not escape the value of the BEAF Slider widget's shortcode field before outputting it on the front end (the value is passed through do_shortcode, which echoes non-shortcode content verbatim), allowing users with administrator-level access to store a script that executes in the browser of any visitor who loads a pa

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-15668: A vulnerability has been found in louisho5 picobot up to 0.2.0. This vulnerabili...
item.description: A vulnerability has been found in louisho5 picobot up to 0.2.0. This vulnerability affects the function WebTool.Execute of the file internal/agent/tools/web.go of the component web Tool. The manipulation of the argument url leads to server-side request forgery. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The project was informed of the proble

item.title: [LOW] CVE-2026-15629: A weakness has been identified in louisho5 picobot up to 0.2.0. Impacted is the ...
item.description: A weakness has been identified in louisho5 picobot up to 0.2.0. Impacted is the function CreateSkill/GetSkill of the file internal/agent/tools/filesystem.go of the component Workspace Handler. Executing a manipulation can lead to link following. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. The project was informed of

item.description: Malicious IP range: 1.10.16.0/20 — SBL256894

item.title: [MEDIUM] Microsoft 365 Services Outage Impacted Teams, Copilot, Purview and Other Services
item.description: A Microsoft 365 outage disrupted access to several widely used enterprise services, including Microsoft Teams, SharePoint Online, OneDrive, Copilot Chat, Microsoft Purview, and Power BI. The incident primarily affected customers whose Microsoft 365 traffic was routed through the North America region. Microsoft tracked the outage under incident ID MO1437424 and said the disruption began at […] The post Microsoft 365 Services Outage Impacted Teams, Copilot, Purview and Other Services appeare

item.title: [MEDIUM] Hackers Allegedly Claim Breach of Decathlon Customer Database With 160 Million Records
item.description: A threat actor is allegedly claiming to possess and sell a Decathlon customer database containing approximately 160 million records. The database was advertised on a cybercrime forum, where the seller stated that payment would be accepted through cryptocurrency. The alleged breach has not been independently verified, and Decathlon has not publicly confirmed that its systems […] The post Hackers Allegedly Claim Breach of Decathlon Customer Database With 160 Million Records appeared first on

item.title: [MEDIUM] ISC Stormcast For Friday, July 24th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/10022, (Fri, Jul 24th)
